headline.purpose: Modal titles and major panel headers.

title.purpose: Section titles, current song names, and active workflow labels.

body.purpose: Standard UI copy and settings explanations.

label.purpose: Status chips, compact metadata, keyboard hints, and control labels.

shadows.name: control-shadow
shadows.purpose: Small tactile confirmation for buttons and inputs.

shadows.name: menu-shadow
shadows.purpose: Context menus, selects, popovers, and tooltips.

shadows.name: modal-shadow
shadows.purpose: Large blocking overlays and confirmation flows.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Default high-confidence action button for dark mode.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 36px; padding: 8px 16px; border: none; border-radius: 6px; background: #fafafa; color: #0a0a0a; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,.2); transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(.25,1,.5,1), transform 150ms cubic-bezier(.25,1,.5,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #e5e5e5; } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: translateY(1px); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 1px solid #a3a3a3; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Secondary action button for toolbar and modal actions.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; height: 36px; padding: 8px 16px; border: 1px solid #262626; border-radius: 6px; background: #262626; color: #fafafa; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; font-weight: 600; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,.2); transition: background-color 150ms cubic-bezier(.25,1,.5,1), border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(.25,1,.5,1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #333333; border-color: #404040; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 1px solid #a3a3a3; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Search Input
components.description: Compact operational field for lyric search and settings values.
components.css: .ds-input-search { height: 36px; width: 240px; border: 1px solid #262626; border-radius: 6px; background: transparent; color: #fafafa; padding: 4px 12px; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif; font-size: 14px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,.16); transition: border-color 150ms cubic-bezier(.25,1,.5,1), box-shadow 150ms cubic-bezier(.25,1,.5,1); } .ds-input-search::placeholder { color: #a3a3a3; } .ds-input-search:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: #a3a3a3; box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px #a3a3a3; }

components.name: Status Chip
components.description: Semantic status chip pairing color with text so state is not color-only.
components.css: .ds-status-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 6px; height: 26px; padding: 0 10px; border: 1px solid rgba(16,185,129,.30); border-radius: 999px; background: rgba(16,185,129,.12); color: #6ee7b7; font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: .04em; text-transform: uppercase; } .ds-status-dot { width: 7px; height: 7px; border-radius: 999px; background: #10b981; }

components.name: Modal Surface
components.description: Large floating surface for purposeful blocking flows.
components.html:
i

Streaming Software Integration

Connect LyricDisplay to OBS, vMix, Wirecast and more.
components.css: .ds-modal { display: flex; gap: 16px; width: 420px; padding: 20px 24px; border: 1px solid #262626; border-radius: 16px; background: #0a0a0a; color: #fafafa; box-shadow: 0 25px 50px rgba(0,0,0,.45); font-family: 'Space Grotesk', sans-serif; } .ds-modal-icon { display: grid; place-items: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; border-radius: 12px; background: rgba(59,130,246,.15); color: #93c5fd; font-weight: 700; flex: 0 0 auto; } .ds-modal h2 { margin: 0; font-size: 20px; line-height: 1.25; font-weight: 600; letter-spacing: -.01em; } .ds-modal p { margin: 6px 0 0; color: #a3a3a3; font-size: 13px; line-height: 1.5; }

narrative.overview: LyricDisplay should feel like a worship-first control surface with the confidence of pro AV software and the restraint of a dark chapel. The app is operated in dim rooms during live services, so dark mode is not cosmetic; it is the physical environment. The interface should lower glare, make live state unmistakable, and keep every control calm under pressure.
keyCharacteristics: Dark, low-glare surfaces for real worship and production rooms.
Dense but disciplined control placement for fast operation.
Status color used only where state matters.
Rounded, familiar controls that feel safe for volunteers.
Overlay depth reserved for modals, menus, and temporary focus.

rules.name: The Low-Glare Rule
rules.body: Dark mode is the primary environment. Never use pure black or pure white in new work; tint neutrals toward the warm chapel surface.

rules.name: The Status Earns Color Rule
rules.body: Signal Blue, Ready Green, Caution Amber, and Stop Rose are for status, focus, and action only. Do not use them as decorative washes.

rules.name: The Console Voice Rule
rules.body: Labels must be short, concrete, and readable at a glance. Do not use display typography inside controls, lists, or dense settings.

rules.name: The Flat-Until-Floating Rule
rules.body: A surface at rest stays flat. If it floats above the live workspace, it earns shadow; if it is just a panel, it earns a border and tonal separation.

dos: Do preserve dark mode as the primary design scene for dim worship and production rooms.
Do use semantic state color only for meaning: Signal Blue for information/current focus, Ready Green for safe/connected, Caution Amber for warning, Stop Rose for danger.
Do keep live-state controls glanceable: output visibility, selected lyric, connection state, active file, and display routing must be obvious.
Do use 1px borders, tonal layers, and compact spacing before adding shadows.
Do keep keyboard focus visible and high contrast on every interactive element.
donts: Don't make LyricDisplay look like a generic SaaS dashboard.
Don't make it gamer or neon dark mode.
Don't use church bulletin clip-art or decorative worship clichés.
Don't make it toy-like volunteer software; volunteer-safe still means serious and precise.
Don't recreate cluttered legacy AV-rack software.
Don't use side-stripe borders greater than 1px as accents on cards, list items, callouts, or alerts.
Don't use gradient text or decorative glassmorphism.
Don't rely on color alone for connection, output, warning, or error states.
